'''Ingoda''', also known as '''Ingoda the Hutt''', was a minor [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] who operated during the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. A brutal being, Ingoda enjoyed accumulating [[Theelin/Legends|Theelins]]—an [[Artist/Legends|artistic]] but increasingly rare [[Species/Legends|species]]—as [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]]. However, Ingoda regularly fell into debt to the more powerful crime [[Lord/Legends|lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] and was forced to sell two of his Theelin slaves, [[Diva Funquita/Legends|Diva Funquita]] and [[Diva Shaliqua/Legends|Diva Shaliqua]], to Jabba to repay his debts.

==Biography==
Ingoda,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|109|Ingoda}}</ref> often called Ingoda the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], was a Hutt entrepreneur<ref name="db" /> and minor [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]]<ref name="CSWE" /> who was active during the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Ingoda made a hobby of "collecting" [[Theelin/Legends|Theelins]]—an increasingly rare [[Near-Human]] [[Species/Legends|species]] noted for its [[Artist/Legends|artistic]] talent—as [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]]. Ingoda also owned slaves of other species, and, unbeknownst to the Hutt, his slaves would mate; two of his slaves produced the Theelin-[[Hybrid/Legends|halfbreed]] [[Diva Shaliqua/Legends|Diva Shaliqua]]. When Ingoda discovered that his slaves were procreating, he was infuriated. Nevertheless, he allowed Shaliqua to live, believing that it would be more advantageous to own and profit from the [[Gender/Legends|female]] halfbreed if she turned out to possess the natural [[Diva|singing faculties]] associated with many Theelin females.<ref name="db" />

However, Ingoda formed a business rivalry with [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]<ref name="db" />—a member of the Hutt [[Desilijic/Legends|Desilijic]] [[Kajidic/Legends|clan]] and a [[Jabba's criminal empire/Legends|powerful]] crime [[Lord/Legends|lord]] operating out of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="Jabba">{{DB|character|jabbathehutt|Jabba the Hutt}}</ref> As a result, Ingoda would often become indebted to the more clever-minded Jabba. When he was forced to pay off his debts to Jabba,<ref name="CSWE" /> Ingoda sold two of his Theelin female slaves, Diva Shaliqua and [[Diva Funquita/Legends|Diva Funquita]], to his business rival.<ref name="db" />

==Personality and traits==
A male Hutt, Ingoda was a cruel being; he made a hobby of accumulating Theelin slaves and was outraged when he discovered that his slaves were reproducing. He let the young Diva Shaliqua live, however, hoping that she would develop into a profitable being in the future.<ref name="db" /> Ingoda's business intelligence was lacking in comparison to those of more infamous Hutt crime lords of his [[Time/Legends|time]], such as Jabba the Hutt, and, as a result, Ingoda would often fall into debt to Jabba.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ingoda was first mentioned in the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry for Diva Shaliqua, published as part of the [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] contest ''[[What's The Story?]]'', which allowed fans to author certain aspects of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' saga. Shaliqua's entry was written by [[Nathan O'Keefe]]—under the alias "Valin Kenobi"—and user "[[Nelwyn53100|newlyn53100]]." Although O'Keefe created the section featuring Ingoda, the concept of Ingoda "collecting" Theelin slaves was added by StarWars.com during the entry's editing process.<ref name="blog">{{Blog|valinkenobi|12|Diva Shaliqua: Behind the scenes}}</ref> Ingoda later received his own entry in the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
